title: React Senior Engineer Assessment
description: Comprehensive evaluation for Senior React roles. Focuses on reconciliation internals, advanced hooks, architectural patterns, and performance bottlenecks.

questions.text: How does React Fiber change the reconciliation process compared to the old stack reconciler?
options: It uses a recursive approach that cannot be interrupted
It enables incremental rendering by breaking work into units called 'fibers'
It removes the need for the Virtual DOM entirely
It forces all updates to be synchronous for better data integrity
questions.explanation: Fiber allows React to pause, reuse, or abort work based on priority, enabling features like Concurrent Mode.

questions.text: What is the primary difference between `useMemo` and `useCallback`?
options: useMemo returns a memoized function, useCallback returns a memoized value
useMemo returns a memoized value, useCallback returns a memoized function
useMemo is for side effects, useCallback is for state
There is no difference; they are aliases
questions.explanation: useMemo caches the result of a function call; useCallback caches the function definition itself.

questions.text: When implementing a 'Portal' in React, what happens to event bubbling?
options: Events bubble through the physical DOM tree
Events bubble through the React component tree (virtual tree)
Event bubbling is disabled for portals
Events only bubble if the portal is attached to the body
questions.explanation: Even though a portal can exist anywhere in the physical DOM, React maintains the event bubbling behavior through the component tree hierarchy.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `useId` hook introduced in React 18?
options: To generate random keys for list items
To generate unique IDs that are stable across server and client
To track the ID of the current user session
To identify specific DOM nodes for testing
questions.explanation: useId creates stable IDs for accessibility attributes, avoiding mismatches during hydration in SSR apps.

questions.text: In a Redux toolkit setup, what is the 'ExtraReducers' field used for?
options: Adding middleware to the store
Handling actions defined outside of the slice (e.g., async thunks)
Creating selectors automatically
Handling local component state
questions.explanation: extraReducers allows a slice to respond to other action types besides those it has generated, commonly used with createAsyncThunk.

questions.text: Which method is the modern replacement for `componentDidCatch` in functional components?
options: useEffect with a try/catch
useErrorBoundary hook
There is currently no Hook equivalent; use a Class ErrorBoundary
useException
questions.explanation: As of now, React does not have a hook equivalent for the getDerivedStateFromError or componentDidCatch lifecycle methods.

questions.text: What does the `startTransition` API do in React 18?
options: It forces an immediate re-render
It marks an update as non-urgent, allowing urgent updates to interrupt it
It creates a CSS transition for the component
It delays the mounting of a component by a fixed time
questions.explanation: Transitions allow you to keep the UI responsive by deprioritizing heavy, non-essential rendering work.

questions.text: How can you prevent a component from re-rendering when a Context value it doesn't use changes?
options: Split the Context into smaller, more specific providers
Use React.memo on the consumer component
Wrap the consumer in useMemo to select specific data
All of the above
questions.explanation: Context optimization usually involves splitting providers or using memoization strategies to prevent unnecessary downstream renders.

questions.text: What is the 'Double Buffer' technique in React Fiber?
options: Storing state in both localStorage and memory
Using a 'current' tree and a 'workInProgress' tree to prepare updates
Rendering two copies of every component for redundancy
A way to handle two simultaneous API calls
questions.explanation: React Fiber uses two trees: one representing the UI currently on screen and another to prepare the next state off-screen.

questions.text: Which Hook should be used if you need to read the layout from the DOM and synchronously re-render?
options: useEffect
useLayoutEffect
useInsertionEffect
useMemo
questions.explanation: useLayoutEffect runs synchronously after DOM mutations, making it ideal for measuring elements before the paint.

options: Injecting dynamic styles for CSS-in-JS libraries
Inserting elements into the DOM
Handling form input insertions
Optimizing list rendering
questions.explanation: useInsertionEffect is specifically for CSS-in-JS authors to inject styles before other effects run.

questions.text: What occurs if you call a state update function with the same value as the current state?
options: React re-renders the component anyway
React bails out without rendering the children or firing effects
React throws a warning
React clears the state
questions.explanation: React uses Object.is comparison; if values are identical, it skips the render process (Bailout).

questions.text: Which pattern is used to pass data through many layers of components without props?
options: Prop Drilling
Render Props
Context API
HOC
questions.explanation: Context provides a way to share values between components without having to explicitly pass a prop through every level.

questions.text: In testing, why would you use `act()` from React Testing Library?
options: To click on elements
To ensure all updates have been processed and applied to the DOM
To mock an API call
To generate a snapshot
questions.explanation: act() guarantees that all updates related to the interaction have been resolved before making assertions.

questions.text: What is the 'Render-as-you-fetch' pattern facilitated by Suspense?
options: Fetching data in useEffect after rendering
Starting to fetch data as soon as possible, before the component even renders
Fetching data on the server only
Waiting for all components to render before fetching
questions.explanation: This pattern allows components to 'suspend' rendering while data is being loaded, reducing waterfalls.
